title: HTML Tokens, Error Recovery, Parser Selection, and Differential Evidence · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers html tokens, error recovery, parser selection, and differential evidence into a changed bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Distinguish source bytes, decoded text, tokens, parse errors, the constructed tree, serialized markup, and browser DOM state.
Predict omitted tags, mismatched nesting, duplicate attributes, character references, templates, foreign content, and recovery effects on target nodes.
Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Token Tree Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab site-quality team handles case 2237 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate malformed HTML recovery, explicit parser identity, script boundaries, and differential tree evidence; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-parser-differential-1gep5j
# Competency: scraper-parser-differential.
# Case 8603: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def evidence_scraper_parser_differential_1gep5j(fixtures: int = 18, tokenized: int = 18, compared_parsers: int = 4, explained_differences: int = 7, executed_scripts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1gep5j = "1gep5j-j5peg1-8603"
    assert evidence_variant_1gep5j
    evidence_axes_1gep5j = (
        "authorization1gep5j",
        "url8603",
        "responsej5peg1",
        "tree1gep5j8603",
        "frontier86031gep5j",
        "reportj5peg18603",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1gep5j)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or tokenized < 0:
        return False, tokenized, "parser-fixtures"
    if tokenized != fixtures:
        return False, tokenized, "parse-reconciliation"
    if compared_parsers < 3 or explained_differences < 3:
        return False, compared_parsers, "parser-differential"
    if executed_scripts > 0:
        return False, executed_scripts, "script-execution"
    assert tokenized == fixtures and executed_scripts == 0
    return True, explained_differences, "explicit-parser-model"
